title: Contact a Local Office for Information
summary: Call or visit a local government or community office to ask for information you need.
learnerPromise: You will be able to reach out to a local office by phone or in person and get the information you are looking for.
whyItMatters: Local offices handle questions about housing, permits, benefits, voting, and community services. Knowing how to contact them gives you access to resources and answers that affect your daily life.
realLifeUses: You need to ask about a permit, license, or local program
You want to find out if you qualify for a community benefit or service
You have a question about trash pickup, water bills, or road repairs
You want to report a problem in your neighborhood and do not know who to call
materialsNeeded: A phone or way to visit in person
A notebook and pen to write down answers
result: You have made contact with a local office and received the information or next steps you needed.
youWillLearn: How to find the right office or department for your question
How to prepare what to say before calling or visiting
How to ask clear questions and write down the answers
How to follow up if you need more help or documents
miniChallenge: Find the phone number of a local office that handles a question you have, call them, and write down the answer or next steps they give you.
steps: Pick a question you have that a local office might answer
Search online or ask someone for the right office and phone number
Write down your question and any details you may need to share
Call or visit the office during open hours and ask your question
Write down the answer, the name of the person you spoke with, and any next steps
completionCriteria: You successfully reached the office by phone or in person
You received an answer or clear next steps for your question
commonProblems: You get sent to the wrong department — ask them to transfer you or give you the right number
The office is closed or busy — try calling at a different time or use email
You do not understand the answer — ask them to repeat it or explain it in simpler terms
tips: Before you call, write your main question on a card or sticky note so you do not forget
Have a pen ready — do not rely on memory for phone numbers or instructions
If visiting in person, bring ID and any related mail or documents with you
